operating_state: Total revenues increased 74% to $10.39 billion driven by 96,464 additional Model 3 and Model Y deliveries from production ramping at Gigafactory Shanghai and Fremont Factory. Energy generation and storage revenue increased 69% from higher solar deployments and storage product sales. Regulatory credit sales contributed $518 million, up $164 million year-over-year despite fewer Model S and Model X deliveries during production transitions.
cost_structure: Total automotive gross margin remained stable at 26% as Model 3 and Model Y margin improvements from localized procurement and manufacturing in China offset lower average selling prices from higher Standard Range variant mix. Energy generation and storage gross margin declined to negative 20% due to higher Solar Roof proportion operating at lower margins during product ramp and increased service maintenance costs.
material_events: Company invested $1.50 billion in bitcoin and began accepting bitcoin payments, realizing $128 million gains on sales while recording $27 million impairment losses. Capital expenditures reached $1.35 billion primarily for Gigafactory Texas and Berlin construction and Shanghai expansion. Early conversions of in-the-money convertible notes required $1.45 billion cash payments.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues creating supply chain challenges including port congestion, intermittent supplier delays and microchip shortages requiring expedited delivery of critical parts. Foreign currency exposure through revenues and costs denominated in multiple currencies remains largely unhedged. Evolving regulatory frameworks for autonomous vehicle deployment across multiple jurisdictions create potential for conflicting or restrictive regulations.
forward_outlook: Gigafactory Berlin and Texas production and deliveries targeted for late 2021 with proprietary battery cell manufacturing to improve vehicle performance and decrease production costs. Company maintains long-term bitcoin holding strategy while developing FSD technology toward full self-driving capability under expanded heavy trucking operations led by Jerome Guillen as President, Tesla Heavy Trucking.
uncertainty_profile: Bitcoin holdings subject to volatile market prices with potential impairment charges creating earnings volatility. Production ramp uncertainties at new international facilities may be exacerbated by concurrent projects and industry-wide component constraints. CEO Performance Award stock compensation expense remains subject to achievement of remaining operational milestones creating potential for significant future catch-up expenses.
